hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2 and TM5 while reducing off-target affinity, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy prioritizes ddG over selectivity, leading to strong binding but insufficient SSTR2 selectivity. Future iterations must focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to improve Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved-pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrogen bonds and electrostatic interactions in off-target pockets (SSTR3/SSTR5) without affecting the native pharmacophore.
